minimum_age.exceptions: No statutory exceptions for emancipated minors

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind, understanding the nature and extent of their property, the natural objects of their bounty, and the disposition being made
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another person may sign in the testator's presence and at the testator's direction

witness_requirements.qualifications: Must be competent witnesses; generally any person who is of sound mind
witness_requirements.presence_rules: Witnesses must sign in the presence of the testator
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness does not invalidate the will, but the interested witness's bequest may be reduced to the intestate share unless there are two other disinterested witnesses

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before a notary public or other authorized officer (Conn. Gen. Stat. § 45a-285)

electronic_wills.notes: Connecticut does not currently have an electronic wills statute

holographic_wills.notes: Holographic wills are not recognized in Connecticut

nuncupative_wills.notes: Oral wills are not recognized in Connecticut

methods: Executing a subsequent will or codicil that revokes the prior will expressly or by inconsistency
Physical act of burning, canceling, tearing, or obliterating the will by the testator or someone in the testator's presence and at their direction

special_provisions: Connecticut is a separate property state. Surviving spouse has a statutory right to elect against the will, receiving a life estate in one-third of the decedent's real and personal property. Connecticut has no forced heirship for children
statute_citations: Conn. Gen. Stat. § 45a-250 (Who may make a will)

minimum_age.exceptions: Emancipated minors may make a will under Maine's UPC framework (18-C MRS § 2-501)

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind, understanding the nature and extent of their property, the natural objects of their bounty, and the disposition being made
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another individual may sign in the testator's conscious presence and by the testator's direction

witness_requirements.qualifications: Must be competent individuals who can observe and attest to the signing
witness_requirements.presence_rules: Each witness must sign within a reasonable time after witnessing either the signing of the will or the testator's acknowledgment of the signature or the will
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness does not invalidate the will under Maine's UPC-based approach

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign an affidavit before an officer authorized to administer oaths (18-C MRS § 2-504)

electronic_wills.notes: Maine does not currently have an electronic wills statute

holographic_wills.notes: Valid whether or not witnessed if the signature and material portions are in the testator's handwriting (18-C MRS § 2-502(b))

nuncupative_wills.notes: Oral wills are not recognized in Maine

methods: Executing a subsequent will that revokes the previous will expressly or by inconsistency
Performing a revocatory act on the will (burning, tearing, canceling, obliterating, or destroying) by the testator or by another in the testator's conscious presence and at their direction

special_provisions: Maine is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share right under 18-C MRS § 2-202. Maine adopted the Uniform Probate Code
statute_citations: 18-C MRS § 2-507 (Revocation by writing or by act)

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind, understanding the nature and extent of their property, the natural objects of their bounty, and the disposition being made
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another person may sign in the testator's conscious presence and by the testator's direction (MGL c. 190B § 2-502)

witness_requirements.presence_rules: Each witness must sign within a reasonable time after witnessing either the signing of the will or the testator's acknowledgment of the signature or the will
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness does not invalidate the will under Massachusetts UPC approach (MGL c. 190B § 2-505)

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign an affidavit before a notary public (MGL c. 190B § 2-504)

electronic_wills.notes: Massachusetts does not currently have an electronic wills statute. During COVID-19, temporary measures allowed remote witnessing but these have expired

holographic_wills.notes: Holographic wills are not recognized in Massachusetts

nuncupative_wills.notes: Oral wills are not recognized in Massachusetts

methods: Executing a subsequent will that revokes the previous will expressly or by inconsistency
Performing a revocatory act on the will by the testator or by another in the testator's conscious presence and at their direction

special_provisions: Massachusetts is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share. Massachusetts adopted the Uniform Probate Code in 2012. Divorce automatically revokes dispositions to the former spouse (MGL c. 190B § 2-804)
statute_citations: MGL c. 190B § 2-501 (Who may make a will)

minimum_age.exceptions: Married minors under 18 may make a will (RSA 551:1)

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind and not under duress or undue influence
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another person may sign in the testator's presence and by the testator's express direction

witness_requirements.presence_rules: Witnesses must sign in the presence of the testator
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: A bequest to an attesting witness is void unless there are two other disinterested witnesses, or the interested witness would have been an heir at law

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before an authorized officer (RSA 551:2-a)

electronic_wills.notes: New Hampshire does not currently have an electronic wills statute

holographic_wills.notes: Holographic wills are not recognized in New Hampshire

nuncupative_wills.notes: Recognized in limited circumstances: soldiers in actual military service and mariners at sea may make nuncupative wills for personal property (RSA 551:16)

methods: Executing a subsequent will or codicil
Physical destruction (burning, tearing, canceling, obliterating) by the testator or someone in the testator's presence and at their direction
Operation of law (divorce revokes dispositions to former spouse)

special_provisions: New Hampshire is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has the right to waive the will and take their intestate share. New Hampshire has no state estate or inheritance tax

minimum_age.exceptions: Emancipated minors may make a will (N.J.S.A. 3B:3-1)

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind, understanding the nature and extent of their property, the natural objects of their bounty, and the disposition being made
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another individual may sign in the testator's conscious presence and by the testator's direction (N.J.S.A. 3B:3-2)

witness_requirements.presence_rules: Each witness must sign within a reasonable time after witnessing either the signing or the testator's acknowledgment
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness does not invalidate the will (N.J.S.A. 3B:3-8)

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit. Required for electronic wills

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before an officer authorized to administer oaths (N.J.S.A. 3B:3-4)

electronic_wills.notes: New Jersey adopted the Uniform Electronic Wills Act. Electronic wills must be in an electronic record, electronically signed by the testator, and witnessed by two persons. Must be notarized

holographic_wills.notes: Valid whether or not witnessed if the signature and material portions are in the testator's handwriting (N.J.S.A. 3B:3-3)

nuncupative_wills.notes: Oral wills are not recognized in New Jersey

methods: Executing a subsequent will that revokes the previous will expressly or by inconsistency
Performing a revocatory act on the will by the testator or by another in the testator's conscious presence and at their direction

special_provisions: New Jersey is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share right of one-third of the augmented estate (N.J.S.A. 3B:8-1). New Jersey eliminated its estate tax effective January 1, 2018

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind and memory: understand the nature and consequences of making a will, know the nature and extent of their property, and know the natural objects of their bounty
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed or printed. Holographic wills only recognized for military/mariners

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another person may sign the testator's name in the testator's presence and at the testator's direction. The testator must sign or acknowledge at the end of the will (EPTL § 3-2.1)

witness_requirements.qualifications: Must be competent persons at least 18 years old
witness_requirements.presence_rules: The testator must declare to each witness that the instrument is their will. Each witness must sign within 30 days of attestation. Witnesses must sign at the testator's request
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: A bequest to an attesting witness is void unless there are two other disinterested witnesses. The interested witness may still serve as a witness, and the rest of the will remains valid (EPTL § 3-3.2)

notarization.notes: Not required. New York uses an attestation clause rather than a self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: New York does not use a traditional self-proving affidavit. A properly drafted attestation clause creates a presumption of due execution (EPTL § 3-2.1). SCPA § 1406 allows witnesses to submit affidavits in lieu of testimony

electronic_wills.notes: New York signed an electronic wills law in 2025 (S2224/A1614), effective December 2027. Will allow electronic execution and remote witnessing via audio-video technology

holographic_wills.notes: Only for: (1) members of the armed forces during a conflict, (2) persons serving with or accompanying armed forces, or (3) mariners at sea. Such wills expire after specific time periods (EPTL § 3-2.2)

nuncupative_wills.notes: Only for the same limited classes as holographic wills: armed forces members and mariners at sea. Must be made during last sickness or in fear of imminent death (EPTL § 3-2.2)

methods: Executing a subsequent will that expressly revokes the prior will
Physical act of burning, tearing, cutting, canceling, obliterating by the testator or by another at the testator's direction and in their presence
A later will revokes a prior will to the extent of inconsistency
revocation.notes: EPTL § 3-4.1. Marriage does not revoke a pre-existing will, but divorce revokes provisions for the former spouse

special_provisions: New York is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has a right of election of the greater of $50,000 or one-third of the net estate (EPTL § 5-1.1-A). New York requires the testator's signature at the end of the will — provisions after the signature are invalid. Publication requirement: testator must declare to witnesses that the instrument is their will
statute_citations: EPTL § 5-1.1-A (Right of election by surviving spouse)

minimum_age.exceptions: Married minors may make a will regardless of age (20 Pa.C.S. § 2501)

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind: able to understand the nature of the act, know the nature and extent of their property, and know the natural objects of their bounty
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another person may sign the testator's name at the testator's direction and in the testator's presence. The signature must be at the end of the will (20 Pa.C.S. § 2502)

witness_requirements.qualifications: Pennsylvania does not require witnesses at execution. Two witnesses are required at probate if the will is not self-proving
witness_requirements.presence_rules: No witness presence rules at execution. At probate, two witnesses must identify the testator's signature
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: Since witnesses are not required at execution, this is generally not an issue

notarization.notes: Not required. A notarized will becomes self-proving and does not need witnesses at probate

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: A will is self-proving if the testator's signature is notarized or if it includes an attestation clause with witnesses' signatures before a notary (20 Pa.C.S. § 3132.1)

electronic_wills.notes: Pennsylvania does not currently have an electronic wills statute

holographic_wills.notes: Effectively recognized because Pennsylvania does not require witnesses at execution. A handwritten, signed will is valid as long as the testator's signature can be proved at probate

nuncupative_wills.notes: Oral wills are not recognized in Pennsylvania

methods: Executing a later will or codicil that expressly or impliedly revokes the prior will
Physical act (burning, tearing, canceling, obliterating, or destroying) by the testator or by another at the testator's direction

special_provisions: Pennsylvania is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share of one-third of certain property (20 Pa.C.S. § 2203). Pennsylvania is unique in not requiring witnesses at the time of will execution — only a signature is needed. Pennsylvania has an inheritance tax (rates vary by relationship to decedent)

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind, understanding the nature and extent of their property, the natural objects of their bounty, and the disposition being made
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another person may sign in the testator's presence and by the testator's express direction

witness_requirements.presence_rules: Witnesses must sign in the presence of the testator (R.I. Gen. Laws § 33-5-5)
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: A bequest to an attesting witness is not automatically void, but may raise questions about undue influence

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before a notary public (R.I. Gen. Laws § 33-7-26)

electronic_wills.notes: Rhode Island does not currently have an electronic wills statute

holographic_wills.notes: Holographic wills are not recognized in Rhode Island

nuncupative_wills.notes: Recognized in limited circumstances: soldiers in actual military service and mariners at sea for personal property only (R.I. Gen. Laws § 33-5-10)

methods: Executing a subsequent will or codicil
Physical destruction (burning, tearing, canceling, obliterating) by the testator or someone in the testator's presence and at their direction

special_provisions: Rhode Island is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share right: life estate in all real property and an outright share of personal property. Rhode Island uses dower and curtesy rights which are largely outdated in most other states
statute_citations: R.I. Gen. Laws § 33-5-2 (Who may make a will)

minimum_age.exceptions: Emancipated minors may make a will (14 V.S.A. § 1)

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound mind, understanding the nature and extent of their property, the natural objects of their bounty, and the disposition being made
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another person may sign in the testator's presence and by the testator's direction

witness_requirements.presence_rules: Witnesses must sign in the presence of the testator AND in the presence of each other — Vermont is one of the few states requiring witnesses to sign in each other's presence
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: A bequest to an attesting witness is void unless there are two other disinterested witnesses to the will

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; recommended for self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before a notary public (14 V.S.A. § 5)

electronic_wills.notes: Vermont does not currently have an electronic wills statute

holographic_wills.notes: Holographic wills are not recognized in Vermont

nuncupative_wills.notes: Oral wills are not recognized in Vermont

methods: Executing a subsequent will or codicil
Physical destruction (burning, tearing, canceling, obliterating) by the testator or someone in the testator's presence and at their direction

special_provisions: Vermont is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share. Vermont requires witnesses to sign in each other's presence, which is stricter than most states. Vermont also recognizes civil unions and extends spousal rights to civil union partners

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be of sound and disposing mind and memory
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another person may subscribe the testator's name at the testator's express direction and in the testator's presence (12 Del. C. § 202)

witness_requirements.qualifications: Must be credible witnesses at least 18 years old
witness_requirements.presence_rules: The testator must sign or acknowledge the will in the presence of the witnesses, and the witnesses must sign in the presence of the testator
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness does not invalidate the will but the bequest to the interested witness may be voided unless there are two other disinterested witnesses

notarization.notes: Not required for validity; used for self-proving affidavit

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Testator and witnesses sign a sworn affidavit before a notary public or other authorized officer (12 Del. C. § 1305)

electronic_wills.notes: Delaware does not currently have an electronic wills statute

holographic_wills.notes: Holographic wills are not recognized in Delaware

nuncupative_wills.notes: Oral wills are not recognized in Delaware

methods: Executing a subsequent will or codicil
Physical destruction (burning, tearing, canceling, obliterating) by the testator or at the testator's direction

special_provisions: Delaware is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has an elective share right of one-third of the elective estate (12 Del. C. § 901). Delaware has no state estate tax for deaths after 2017
statute_citations: 12 Del. C. § 201 (Who may make a will)

testamentary_capacity: The testator must be legally competent, of sound and disposing mind, capable of executing a valid deed or contract
writing_requirement: Must be in writing. Can be typed, printed, or handwritten

signature_requirement.proxy_signing_rules: Another person may sign in the testator's presence and by the testator's express direction

witness_requirements.presence_rules: Witnesses must sign in the testator's presence (Md. Code, Est. & Trusts § 4-102)
witness_requirements.interested_witness_rules: An interested witness may serve as a witness but any bequest to that witness is void to the extent it exceeds the intestate share, unless there are two other disinterested witnesses

notarization.notes: Not required for validity. Maryland relies on witness testimony under oath rather than traditional self-proving affidavits

self_proving_affidavit.requirements: Maryland does not use the traditional self-proving affidavit. Witnesses must testify under oath or provide affidavits during probate

electronic_wills.notes: Maryland recognizes electronic wills with a supervising attorney requirement (Md. Code, Est. & Trusts § 4-104.2)

holographic_wills.notes: Only for members of the armed forces in actual military service or mariners at sea. Valid for one year after discharge (Md. Code, Est. & Trusts § 4-103)

nuncupative_wills.notes: Oral wills are not recognized in Maryland

methods: Executing a subsequent will or codicil
Physical act of burning, canceling, tearing, or obliterating by the testator or at the testator's direction and in the testator's presence
Operation of law (divorce revokes dispositions to former spouse)

special_provisions: Maryland is a separate property/common law state. Surviving spouse has a statutory share: if surviving minor children, one-third; otherwise one-half up to a statutory maximum. Maryland has a state estate tax with a $5 million exemption. Maryland does not use the standard self-proving affidavit format
statute_citations: Md. Code, Est. & Trusts § 4-101 (Who may make a will)
Md. Code, Est. & Trusts § 4-102 (Execution of wills)
Md. Code, Est. & Trusts § 4-103 (Holographic wills — military)
